original_hypothesis.title: MagicSpend EntryPoint funding is incorrectly implemented: entryPointDeposit() sends ETH to EntryPoint address instead of calling depositTo(), risking funds loss/non-accounting
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: MagicSpend.entryPointDeposit() uses `SafeTransferLib.safeTransferETH(entryPoint(), amount)` rather than calling the EntryPoint's `depositTo(paymaster)` method. On EntryPoint v0.6, simply sending ETH to the EntryPoint contract address does not necessarily credit the paymaster's deposit balance (and may revert if EntryPoint lacks a payable receive), leading to operational loss of funds or inability to sponsor gas despite apparent funding.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a concrete code defect in a privileged operational workflow (funding the paymaster). It can cause real incidents: deposits not credited, stranded ETH, paymaster downtime, or confusion during emergency funding.
preconditions: Operator uses entryPointDeposit() to fund EntryPoint deposit for MagicSpend
EntryPoint v0.6 requires depositTo() for accounting (to be confirmed against deployed bytecode on target chain)
EntryPoint does not credit msg.value to MagicSpend deposit via plain ETH transfer

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Funding function sends ETH, but does not specify beneficiary/paymaster; EntryPoint deposits are typically mapped by address and require explicit depositTo.
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Only owner can call, but defect manifests during normal operations.
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Any time operator funds via this method.

operational_questions: Does the canonical EntryPoint at 0x5FF1... accept plain ETH transfers and credit them to sender/paymaster?
Has this function been used in production, and if so, is there evidence of uncredited deposits?
Is there an alternative operational path used to fund deposits (direct depositTo calls) making this function unused/dead?
validation_requirements: Fork test: call entryPointDeposit(amount) and compare EntryPoint.balanceOf(MagicSpend) before/after
Confirm EntryPoint ABI/bytecode semantics on each target chain
Review operational runbooks for paymaster funding

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: If exploitable, attacker could induce operator error by causing paymaster depletion and prompting emergency deposit via this function.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Paymaster remains unfunded; transactions fail; potential cascading outage.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Follow-on attacks: force users into alternative paymasters or fail critical operations.
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: None for defect manifestation; attacker influence is optional.

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Paymaster downtime; stranded ETH; failed sponsored transactions.
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Medium; requires manual recovery if EntryPoint holds uncredited ETH or if transaction reverted unexpectedly.
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: All gas sponsorship and user withdrawal workflows relying on paymaster liquidity.

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: entryPointDeposit() calls; mismatch between EntryPoint ETH balance changes and MagicSpend deposit changes
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Reported 'funded but still out of deposit' incidents

validation_result.impact_summary: No impact - the canonical EntryPoint v0.6 at the hardcoded address has receive() { depositTo(msg.sender); }, so plain ETH transfers DO correctly credit the sender's deposit balance.
validation_result.detailed_analysis:
The hypothesis claims that MagicSpend.entryPointDeposit() is defective because it uses SafeTransferLib.safeTransferETH() (plain ETH transfer) instead of calling IEntryPoint.depositTo(). The core precondition of this hypothesis is that 'EntryPoint does not credit msg.value to MagicSpend deposit via plain ETH transfer.' This precondition is provably false for the canonical EntryPoint v0.6.

The canonical EntryPoint v0.6 at address 0x5FF137D4b0FDCD49DcA30c7CF57E578a026d2789 inherits from StakeManager, which implements:
